metadata.notes: agent_supports: yes; daemon_capability: bug (parser skips the type:"info" cancel notice → sticks in working); driver_capability: gap:interrupt

assessment.body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: yes
daemon_capability: bug; driver_capability: gap:interrupt

Route: driver-gap (record ports the `interrupt` step, then drives; because daemon=bug the recording is expected to leave the session STUCK in working, so it lands known_failing).

## Scenario

user-esc-interrupt: the user cancels a long in-flight turn with ESC. User-observable signal (acceptance_criteria + process): ready → working → ready; state does NOT stick in working post-interrupt; a follow-up prompt drives a clean ready → working → ready recovery arc proving the parser reset.

## agent_supports: yes

Gemini CLI binds ESC to cancel the in-flight turn. The cancel path runs through the interactive UI's `handleUserCancelledEvent` (bundle interactiveCli-OYDA765S.js): on the abort signal the stream yields `GeminiEventType.UserCancelled` (`processGeminiStreamEvents` returns status 1), the handler flushes any pending history item (a partial `gemini` message, or a `tool_group` whose validating/scheduled/awaiting_approval/executing tools are rewritten to status `cancelled`), then appends `addItem({ type: "info", text: "User cancelled the request." })`, calls `setIsResponding(false)`, and returns control to the prompt. Only the in-flight turn aborts; the process stays alive. So ESC cancellation is a real, shipped feature (v0.46.0).

## daemon_capability: bug

A durable trace of the cancel exists on disk but the gemini-cli parser mis-handles it, so the session sticks in `working` indefinitely — the same failure mode as turn-aborted-by-error:

- What lands on disk: the `useHistoryManager.addItem` chat-recording switch records ONLY `compression`/`info`/`warning`/`error` items to the session file via `chatRecordingService.recordMessage`, and explicitly BREAKS (no-op) for `user`/`gemini`/`gemini_content` (bundle chunk-SCXTH56Q.js, `switch (itemData.type)` under `if (!isResuming && chatRecordingService)`). So the cancel writes exactly one MessageRecord — `{type:"info",content:"User cancelled the request."}` — to ~/.gemini/tmp/<project>/chats/session-*.jsonl.
- No terminal `gemini` message is written for the cancelled turn: on UserCancelled `processGeminiStreamEvents` returns 1 → the caller early-`return`s BEFORE the normal post-stream `recordMessage({type:"gemini", content: responseText})` flush (interactiveCli-OYDA765S.js). The pending partial `gemini` item flushed via `addItem` during cancel is NOT recorded (the switch breaks for `gemini`). So the last persisted observable event for the turn remains the user prompt (`user_message`, state=working).
- The parser drops the cancel notice: `parser.go` `parseMessage` dispatches strictly on type — `user`→parseUser, `gemini`→parseAssistant, **default → return false → ev.Skip** (parser.go:100-109, 63-65). `type:"info"` (and `error`/`warning`) all fall through to default and are silently skipped.
- `turn_done` is inferred ONLY from a final text-only `gemini` message with no open tool calls (parser.go:208-212); a cancelled turn never produces that message. `IsAgentDone()` fires only on LastEventType `turn_done` (gemini's path) or the claudecode-only `assistant`/`assistant_output` fallback (session.go) — neither fires. There is NO inactivity-driven working→ready sweep (the stale-tool timer was deleted in PR #106). So nothing settles the session.
- The claudecode ESC mechanism does NOT apply here: the gemini parser never sets `ev.IsUserInterrupt`, so classifier rule 3 (LastWasUserInterrupt + LastEventType=user + !HasOpenToolCall → ready, state_classifier.go:80-95) cannot fire for gemini-cli.

The fix is local and small (map the cancel `type:"info"` notice — or treat info/error/warning notices generally — to a terminal/turn_done signal in the gemini parser, the same shape as the opencode `message.data.error` fix and the gemini turn-aborted-by-error fix). Signal present, daemon mis-handles → `bug`, NOT `incapable` (no architecture limit; the trace is on disk).

Note on the tool-cancel sub-case: if ESC lands while a tool is open, the flushed `tool_group` marks tools `cancelled`; gemini also persists a finished toolCall with a terminal status, and the parser's `case "error","cancelled"` (parser.go:197-202) closes the call (ToolResultIDs) and sets IsError. That balances HasOpenToolCall but still does not emit turn_done, so the session still sticks in working — the bug is the same. This cell exercises the pure-text-stream ESC flavour (no tool open), matching the canonical claudecode spec.

## driver_capability: gap:interrupt

The recipe needs an `interrupt` step (send ESC to the in-flight turn). The gemini-cli interactive driver implements only `send`/`slash`/`wait_turn`/`sleep`; the `interrupt` case is a loud `not_implemented` stub (`not_implemented interrupt || break` with `# TODO(gemini-cli): Escape/Ctrl-C the in-flight turn`, driver-interactive.sh:235; the helper exits 3). The recipe-lint helper lists `interrupt` as an arm only because the case label exists — the body is a stub, so the step type is NOT actually implemented → `gap:interrupt`. This is tooling work (port the step, e.g. `tmux send-keys -t "$SESSION" Escape`), not an observability limit. Same gap opencode and kiro-cli carry for this scenario.

## known_failing

This cell records `known_failing`: the spec asserts the CORRECT contract (ready→working→ready, no stick) while the live recording — once the interrupt step is ported — is expected to leave the session STUCK in working until the parser fix lands. Do not weaken the spec to match the bug.
caveats: daemon=bug is grounded in code + the bundle, not a live recording: the cancel-notice trace (type:"info") and the early-return that suppresses the terminal gemini message are read off interactiveCli-OYDA765S.js / chunk-SCXTH56Q.js and parser.go. The exact on-disk shape (whether ANY partial gemini text is also flushed before the early return on a slow stream) is a property of what the agent WRITES and is provisional until record promotes it; confidence is held at 0.75 for this reason.

Two sub-cases share the cancel path: ESC during pure text streaming (this cell — only the type:"info" notice lands) and ESC during an open tool call (the tool is also rewritten to status:"cancelled"). Both stick in working under today's parser; the tool sub-case additionally closes the open tool via parser.go:197.
Driver gap is real but cheap to close: record ports a single `interrupt` arm (tmux send-keys Escape) from the reference driver before it can drive this cell at all.

sources.note: handleUserCancelledEvent (v0.46.0): on ESC/abort flushes the pending item then addItem({type:"info",text:"User cancelled the request."}); processGeminiStreamEvents returns UserCancelled(1) → caller early-returns before the post-stream recordMessage({type:"gemini"}) flush.

useHistoryManager.addItem chat-recording switch: records only compression/info/warning/error via chatRecordingService.recordMessage; user/gemini/gemini_content explicitly break (no-op). So the cancel persists exactly one {type:"info"} MessageRecord; no gemini message for the cancelled turn.

parseMessage dispatches strictly on type — user/gemini handled, default (incl. info/error/warning) → return false → ev.Skip (lines 100-109, 63-65). turn_done inferred only from a final text-only gemini message with no open tool calls (208-212). Cancel notice is dropped → no turn_done → no settle.

Rule 2 settles on IsAgentDone (turn_done) only; rule 3 (user ESC interrupt) needs LastWasUserInterrupt, which the gemini parser never sets; rule 4 default → working. No inactivity sweep. So a cancelled gemini turn has no path to ready.
interrupt step is a not_implemented stub (line 235, TODO: Escape/Ctrl-C the in-flight turn); only send/slash/wait_turn/sleep are real → driver=gap:interrupt.

Sibling daemon=bug cell with the identical root cause: the gemini parser skips non-user/gemini MessageRecords (type:"error" there, type:"info" here), so no turn_done fires and the session sticks in working.

preconditions: gemini CLI on PATH and authenticated (oauth-personal or API key)
gemini version where ESC cancels the in-flight turn (confirmed v0.46.0)
the interactive driver's `interrupt` step is ported (currently a not_implemented stub — record must port a tmux send-keys Escape arm before driving)
irrlichd is recording (run-cell.sh isolated, or --attach against a daemon started with --record)
setup: Launch gemini interactively under tmux (gemini -y) so the process outlives the daemon observation window — NOT headless gemini -p, which exits at turn end
Empty cwd under <staging>/cwd/ so the session is fresh

script.text: Count slowly from 1 to 100, with a brief one-sentence explanation for each number. Take your time and stream the response.
Reply with exactly the word: ok

verify: events.jsonl contains a pid_discovered for the launcher bound by cwd shortly after launch
events.jsonl contains a transcript_new / session in ready shortly after launch
events.jsonl contains a state_transition to working after the long prompt lands
transcript session-*.jsonl contains a user-cancelled trace (type:"info" 'User cancelled the request.' MessageRecord) for the cancelled turn

KNOWN_FAILING: events.jsonl is EXPECTED to NOT contain a working→ready settle for the cancelled turn (parser skips type:"info" → no turn_done) — the session sticks in working until the recovery prompt
the recovery prompt drives a clean ready→working→ready arc (proving the parser reset once a terminal gemini message lands)
